questions.question: What is light?
questions.answer: Light is a form of energy that enables us to see objects around us.

questions.question: Name two natural sources of light.
questions.answer: The Sun and stars are natural sources of light.

questions.question: Name two artificial sources of light.
questions.answer: Electric bulbs and candles are artificial sources of light.

questions.question: What is reflection of light?
questions.answer: Reflection of light is the bouncing back of light when it strikes a surface such as a mirror.

questions.question: What is a plane mirror?
questions.answer: A plane mirror is a flat mirror that reflects light and forms images.

questions.question: State two characteristics of images formed by a plane mirror.
questions.answer: Images formed by a plane mirror are upright and of the same size as the object.

questions.question: What is lateral inversion?
questions.answer: Lateral inversion is the sideways reversal of an image formed in a plane mirror.

questions.question: Explain why we can see ourselves in a mirror.
questions.answer: We can see ourselves in a mirror because light from our body reflects off the mirror and enters our eyes.

questions.question: What is a concave mirror?
questions.answer: A concave mirror is a mirror that curves inward and can focus light rays.

questions.question: What is a convex mirror?
questions.answer: A convex mirror is a mirror that curves outward and spreads light rays.

questions.question: Give one use of a concave mirror.
questions.answer: Concave mirrors are used as shaving mirrors or makeup mirrors.

questions.question: Give one use of a convex mirror.
questions.answer: Convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors in vehicles.

questions.question: Explain why convex mirrors are used as rear-view mirrors.
questions.answer: Convex mirrors provide a wider field of view, allowing drivers to see more area behind the vehicle.

questions.question: What is a lens?
questions.answer: A lens is a transparent curved piece of glass or plastic that refracts light.

questions.question: Name the two main types of lenses.
questions.answer: Convex lens and concave lens are the two main types of lenses.

questions.question: What is a convex lens?
questions.answer: A convex lens is a lens that is thicker in the middle and converges light rays.

questions.question: What is a concave lens?
questions.answer: A concave lens is a lens that is thinner in the middle and diverges light rays.

questions.question: Give one use of a convex lens.
questions.answer: Convex lenses are used in magnifying glasses.

questions.question: Give one use of a concave lens.
questions.answer: Concave lenses are used in spectacles to correct short-sightedness.

questions.question: Explain how a magnifying glass works.
questions.answer: A magnifying glass uses a convex lens to produce a larger image of an object.

questions.question: Explain how mirrors help us see objects.
questions.answer: Mirrors reflect light from objects into our eyes, allowing us to see their images.

questions.question: Explain why lenses are important in optical devices.
questions.answer: Lenses are used to focus light in devices such as cameras, microscopes and telescopes.

questions.question: Explain why light is important for vision.
questions.answer: Light reflects from objects and enters our eyes, enabling us to see them.

questions.question: Explain how mirrors and lenses are useful in everyday life.
questions.answer: Mirrors and lenses are used in vehicles, spectacles, cameras and many devices to control and reflect light.

questions.question: Explain the importance of studying light and optical devices.
questions.answer: Studying light helps us understand vision, develop optical instruments and improve technology.
